No matter where you are today in your business and in your life, you're just one funnel away. Hey, this is Russell again. Welcome back to the Funnel Hacker Cookbook. I hope you enjoyed video number one. We had a chance to talk about funnel structure and funnel strategy. We also talked about all the different elements that go inside the funnel. And today's video, we're going to be going into the actual pages that are inside the Funnel Hacker Cookbook, all the elements that go into those pages, what the pages look like, how they work, and it's going to be a lot of fun. So sit back, relax, enjoy this video. So the second section here are what we call the pages. Assume that the pages are similar to recipes. The page, excuse me, the page recipe, the page is the cake, right? So I'm building a cake. So each of these right here is a different type of page. Each one of these is a different type of cake. So if I want to build this presale page, there is a recipe that works for this page. Okay? If I want to build a squeeze page, there's a different recipe for this. It includes different ingredients. In fact, you can see little things. For squeeze page, I need headline, boom, boom, that's the little images of what you need for that type of page. So if you notice all these, it has what ingredients go inside each one of these different pages. And it's different for all of them. Okay? And so that's kind of works. Each of these is the cake. So I'm going to walk through each of these pages because if you understand each of these pages and the strategy behind them, then it'll be really easy when you're like, okay, I need this, I need this. And we'll show you kind of how this works with the Legos here in a minute. There's a lot of foundational stuff we're going over to like lay the foundation and at the end of it, you're going to be like, oh my gosh, this is how all the Legos build together. Okay? All right, so the pages. So a couple of things I understand about pages. When you're working inside the ClickFunnels editor, basically this is the page. This is the hierarchy of how the pages work. Okay? You don't have to be a website designer to understand ClickFunnels. You just have to understand this. There are sections. And then inside of sections, you put rows and columns. Inside of rows and columns, you put elements. That's the magic of ClickFunnels. That's it. So let me break this down real easy. If you're creating a page, think about most pages you've seen. This is what they look like. There's sections, right? There's a top section. I have a logo navigation. There's the middle section. It's got the training or the video or the sales thing. And then there's a bottom section for a footer, right? So you start looking at every single website. It's funny because I looked at web pages forever. My whole life, and I just thought it was just a blah, a bunch of stuff. And so that understood this is how developers and designers design stuff. I was like, oh my gosh, this is easy. All I need is section here, section here, section here. Okay? So sections are green. You'll notice when you're inside of ClickFunnels, sections are green. Now inside of the sections, what we're going to do next is then we're going to add rows. So I'm going to add a row inside of this section here. I'm going to add a row with two columns inside of this one here. I'm going to add a row inside the section here. Okay? And now the last step is then I just plug elements into the rows. So inside this row, I could put an element for an image and a navigation bar. This one here, I put an image, an element of a video maybe. Right here, it could be an order form. Right here, it could be my logo. But that's it. So these elements, these are the ingredients that create the page. But it's super simple. You understand? It's like, this is all it is. This green thing is the sections. Then I set the rows, and then I'm going to figure out what elements, what ingredients pop into each of the sections, and boom, you got a page. And if you got a page, then it's easy to get a funnel. Okay? That's the magic, you guys. Green, blue, orange, sections, rows, elements. I want you to, as you're thinking about this, when you start looking at any website, start looking at that, when you go to eBay next time, they're like, okay, there's their top section, there's their middle section, there's their footer section. Look at any of the websites you go to, any of the products and services you buy, you'll notice, you're like, okay, there's their top section, there's their middle. And then you look at that, okay, inside of there, is there one row? Is there a row of two columns? Like, what does that look like? And it just becomes really simple. And you guys, like, this is how the entire internet is built. And it's really that simple. And for years, the tech guys, they knew this, and they had to do with all their little geeky code stuff that we couldn't figure out, but ClickFunnels made it easy where we were just like, I want a section, boom, boom, there's a section. Like, oh, crap, that was fast. I want a row inside of you, click a button, like, oh, I actually want to row with two columns. Delete it, do the next thing, and like that fast, you can create stuff. Like let's say you own a door to door sales company, you hired a really bad sales person who spoke no English and had him go knock on the door, knock on the door and be like, you know, like vacuum, you want to vacuum? Nobody's going to buy it. Most people's funnels look like. Like they have like, you should buy this cool thing and like they're bad at selling. Like pages still have to sell. The funnel by itself is magic and it's sell it for you. It's the words on the page, the words in the videos, like all those things like the copy called copy. The copy is what sells it. Okay, you get a really good sales person, you get a really good script, they start knocking on doors, they'll close one out of three doors on a vacuum that they don't need, right? Because they understand the words and how to pitch and how to sell it the right way. And so that, inside of here, we also have a funnel scripts is another software program we have that you can get. It says, okay, for this page, these are the funnel scripts you would use. So you need a headline, so you'd use the killer headline wizard. For the video, you need, the video I recommend here is the who, what, why, how video. Here you need the Brunson bullet script. Here you need, so like, these are all the scripts that you would use inside of funnel scripts to build this page. If you're a good copywriter, you can go do it on your own or you can hire a copywriter or if you want to save a ton of time, energy and headache and money, you can use funnel scripts. You literally log in and software asks you five or six questions about you and your business and your product. You click a button and it pops out all the copy and you just copy and paste it in here. And now you have a funnel that's actually set up to convert and actually sell things. So these little hints of what scripts you need in each page are also in there as well, which helps make the process really simple as well. All right, now as we dig into this, there are basically, there are 10 types of pages here inside of the cookbook that we're going to be talking about. The first one here, these are the pre-sell pages. This is like the pre-frame bridge that we talked about. These are the pre-sell pages. Number two are the opt-in pages. There's four types of opt-in pages we can talk about. Number three are the thank you pages. There's different ways to do thank you pages, different recipes for this too. And then there are some other pages that kind of don't really fit in here, but we use them for just different random things I made in other categories as well. Again, this comes back to what we talked about here, seven phases of funnel. So just kind of think through this as we're going through there. You get pre-sell pages with a pre-frame bridge. Opt-in pages are over here and on and on from there. Okay. All right. So pre-sell pages, the first thing we're going to talk about. So the first one is just basically a pre-sell page. So the first type of pre-sell page is a pre-sell page. Now this is an example of a pre-sell page that I like. This is Andre Chaperone has some of the best pre-sell pages I've ever seen. It's super long. So I've chopped it in three columns so you can kind of see the gist of it. But basically it's a page that he sends people who have no idea who he is. Because sometimes if you send someone directly to an order form and they don't know who you are, they're like, I don't know, this person is asking for money already and they're going to run away from you, right? So pre-sell page is all about warming somebody up to getting them to like you. So Andre in this example writes a really good page that builds connection with him, gives people to know who he is, gets them to like him and at the end of it he says, click here to continue page number two, they click there and now it takes them over to one of these other pages in his funnel. So it's a pre-sell page to pre-warm up your relationship with that person. This is an example of a pre-sell page we had back in the day. We had a product called thedarksided.com secrets which was kind of fun. It was like a black hat, how do you have stuff, way back in the day. And it was the same thing. It was a pre-sell page like, hey, there's a video I got on the next page. It's really cool, but you may not want to use it. And this is kind of like the negatives about it. It's like if you want to know what it is, click here and people click there and then they were pre-sealed before they took them. I believe I took them to a video sales letter on the next page. So I was pre-selling them that way. So that's what a pre-sell page is and they're really, really cool and powerful and I love them. All right. Page type number two here is called an article page. An article page is very, very, very, very similar to a pre-sell page. The difference is that typically you create an article site that looks like a Newsy article looking site. Right? So this is an example. One of my friends pages he calls it the fat loss reporter. So it looks like it's more of a news page as opposed to just like a random page that you set up in a funnel, right? And same thing, it's got an article here that just says really cool, powerful article. It's awesome. And the end of it is like, hey, go check out this video to get more. And you can drive traffic really easy from Facebook or other places to go read this really cool article. And the article pre-frames them, gets them all warmed up and then they click on here and then sends them into your funnel. And it's a great way to increase the conversion rate on the next page. And that's kind of what an article page is. All right. The next type of page here is what we call a quiz page. So here is a quiz page. It's also typically something you send someone to at the very beginning. There's two types of quiz pages. And these are some of my favorite ones. So this one right here is a more common, this is one of my favorite quiz pages. I love it. Basically, it does is you come to this quiz page and it's going to take you through a process. Right? So it's like, okay, which one of these foods do you frequently eat? And you answer the question and it takes you to the next question and it keeps going deeper and deeper and deeper. There's five or six questions that goes through. Now the job of this quiz is to figure out who is actually coming to my website. A lot of times we get all sorts of people and we jam them down the exact same funnel. Right? One powerful thing a quiz page can do is help you figure out who's actually coming here and then you can send them to different funnels based on who they are. One good example is I had a friend who was selling a skin cream for women and he was selling it. It was doing really, really well but it wasn't like blowing up like he wanted it to. And one day he was leaving the office kind of frustrating. He was walking out. He heard his receptionist or his support person on the phone trying to explain to this guy like how the cream worked and what it did. He sat there and kind of listened and was like, what in the world is happening? And he realized really quickly this was a man calling. He had no idea how this cream worked. What it even was and the secretary was trying to explain it to him. So when they got the phone he asked, he said, how many men call? And he said, we get 10 to 15 men every single day calling. They're so confused. They want to get this cool gift for their spouse but they don't even know what it is or how it works. He's like, how interesting. So what do you do? He set up a really simple quiz page. He just said, hey are you a man or are you a woman? That was it. And they clicked on the man. He took a new page and said, hey you man, I need to explain how this product works for your spouse. And there were women and it said, hey and it sold to them as a woman. And just that one thing tripled his business just by diverting the traffic each way. So quizzes are really, really cool. This is powerful because in this process he can figure out like is this person too acidic or are they not acidic or are they fine? Like what's the real thing they're struggling with? And they take them to a different funnel based on that. Most of the powerful things you can do with quiz pages. The other thing, this kind of quiz page right here is a very, very, very, very simple quiz page. It's not doing anything outside of just engaging someone. So it's kind of like, you'll see it looks like a squeeze page but it all says, hey how did you hear about us? On TV, down in Canada, email newsletter or other. Just a couple different options and people like other or TV or whatever and they click that and they click next and that's all the quiz does. It literally just gets them to take an action. And I get people all the time like, well where does this store the data for this? I'm like, you don't need the data. It's not about storing the data for these basic quizzes. The goal of this is just to get people engaged. Back in the direct response days in magazine ads they found by having a little thing that said cut out this ad and mail it back to me. Dramatically increase conversions because people had to actually do something. Say, someone comes here and just by them clicking on it, they're likely to be converting on the next page dramatically increases. So these are very, very simple quizzes. So both of these work. Both of these are elements you can build inside of ClickPhone. ClickPhone is really easy on a page and that's what a quiz page is. The last type of pre-sell page is what we call a ClickPop page. And a ClickPop is basically just a pop-up. And we wanted to, didn't want to call it pop-up, because everyone does, we started calling them ClickPops. Now ClickPops are really cool. Someone clicks on a button and a thing pops up and asks for the email address. Now ClickPops are good because you can put them anywhere. If you have a blog, it's a good spot to have a ClickPop or someone clicks on a button and it pops up. They put their email address in and then they're taken into your funnels. And so that's kind of what they are. They're just some examples of different ones. They can be very simple. They can be more complex. But it's basically, it's almost like a squeeze page, but it's somewhere external. So when someone clicks on it, it pops up. They put the email address in and it takes them into your funnels. That's what a ClickPop page is. Alright. So those are the four types of pre-sell pages that we're covering today. The next step I want to talk about are opt-in pages. This is where somebody is going to qualify themselves as a subscriber. They're actually going to give you their email address in exchange for more information. Okay? And so we go through these. The first one is what we call a squeeze page. Different people have different definitions how they kind of categorize these. This is how I look at it, how I explain it. But a lot of people use squeeze page interchangeably for a lot of different things. For me, what a squeeze page is, this type of cake, this type of product, this type of page is basically the goal of it is to not, I'm not giving them anything up front. You'll see that we deal with things on lead magnets and other pages. The goal of this page is just to cause as much curiosity as possible. Okay? I'm not saying I'm going to give you this in exchange for email address or anything. I'm just saying, hey, want to learn how to generate this many leads? Give me your email address and I'll tell you how. Okay? This page is one of the highest-growing pages I've ever seen. Again, it's just like pure rock curiosity. Like warning, these strategies you're about to discover can produce life-changing results at your own risk. Like free video reveals a weird strategy, blah, blah, blah, blah. Give me your email address. Okay? So I'm just trying to cause as much curiosity as possible. Typically, my squeeze pages are a headline and an opt-in box and that's it. Right? And that's kind of my definition of a squeeze page. My goal is to be, give as much curiosity as possible because that'll increase the conversion rates of people who give me their email address. Okay? And that's kind of how a squeeze page works. All right. The next type of page is what we call a reverse squeeze page. So reverse squeeze page is similar to a squeeze page. The difference is on a reverse squeeze page instead of trying to give them as much curiosity as possible to try to get as many emails as I can, I kind of flip that on top. Instead, they have to watch a training video. This is one of Frank Kern did, this is one of Ryan Dice did where you watch a video. It could be 10, 20, 30, 40. It doesn't matter. You should give me a training video first and the end of it, you're like, hey, if you like this stuff, give me your email address. Okay? That's a reverse squeeze page. I'm training them first and then asking for the email address. Whereas a squeeze page, I'm getting their stuff first and I'm going to train them or do whatever on the next page. Okay? And the pros and the cons. The pros of a reverse squeeze page is the people who opt-in are going to be way more highly qualified because they sat through a video for 20, 30 minutes of your training. That's awesome. Your conversion rate will be dramatically lower. Okay? Way less people are going to give you their name and email address, but the people who do are like the highest caliber, the highest quality. So that's kind of the pros and the cons of a reverse squeeze page. I love using these because that's one of the best ways to kind of pre-frame build connections with them before you get them into your funnel. So that's what a reverse squeeze page is. All right. The next one here is what we call a lead magnet. I call, my definition of a lead magnet is where I'm trying to trade somebody something. Okay? So for example here, this is one of Brandon Bouchardt. He's like, hey, I've got this one page productivity planner. You give me your email address, I'll give you this in exchange. So he's trading something, right? It's a lead magnet. Okay? This thing is going to suck you in your lead magnet. This one I had before, I had all these 14 different funnels I had back in the day and I was like, give me your email address, I'll give you all my funnel diagrams. Okay? That's something I did in the day. So it's kind of finding like something you've got, you can trade something like, hey, I'll give you this really cool thing I created, but you got to give me email address first. Okay? It's a really good way to get leads and I call that a lead magnet. Okay? And then the last type of opt-in page here is a coupon page. A coupon page is honestly a very similar to lead magnets, but I created this one more so for local businesses. Sometimes they struggle figuring out, what's my lead magnet? Like I own a restaurant, things like that. And so the example I have here is actually from a daily deal style site where I'm trading a coupon, like hey, give me your email address, I'm going to give you a $50 discount on dinner at my restaurant or things like that. So it's kind of like a lead magnet. It's just based on more of a coupon and that's the lead magnet, it's an actual coupon and this is more traditional for more traditional style businesses. That's what a coupon page works for. Next up are the thank you pages. So after somebody goes through and they give you their email address, then you take them somewhere, right? Sometimes you take them to, there's a lot of places you can take them, but most of the time I'm going to take them to some type of thank you page, not always, but sometimes I will. So the first type of thank you page is just basically a thank you page. And this is really simple. Basically like hey, thanks. Really easy. So there's Amy saying thanks, there's Dan saying thanks and I'm like oh by the way come and follow me or whatever, but it's just really simple. Hey thanks so much, that's it. Okay, the most basic kind that we use all the time. And the next one is probably one of my favorites, it's called an offer wall. I traditionally use these after somebody buys a product for me. The last page is also a thank you page. I love using this one because I know that people are buying that I like to continue to buy stuff. So an offer wall is basically they bought something at the end of it and I say hey thanks so much for buying, you guys are awesome. By the way, here's a bunch of my other products. And these lead now to different, to other funnels I have. So every thank you page I have after somebody buys something, I always have an offer wall. It's like hey you like this, you may like this, this and this. But Amazon does this all the time. By somebody at Amazon they take you to offer wall like oh other people bought this book also bought these five and you're like oh that book does look awesome. It increases how much money they're making every single thing. So an offer wall is basically that. Same telling them thank you and then offering them links to all your other front end products. This is one of mine I think I have, so I'm going to trade with Ellen's on his same thing he's got thank you page and he's like oh here's all my other front end funnels they bring people into to increase how much money he's making. So that is an offer wall page. The next one, this one is one of the most powerful ones that most people don't understand at all it's called the bridge page. So the bridge page is if you're trying to bridge the gap between where somebody is and where they're trying to go. One of the places I use this the most is in network marketing. So network market what will happen is somebody will come like let's, I'm not going to say any specific company, say there's a company right and you've got the company they usually have a corporate website and you can't control the website. Basically the buying experience on the corporate website is horrible. It's like the worst thing on planet earth. So you send people there and it's like slamming them into a brick wall again right and they fall away. And so bridge page is something I put in between there and it's a really simple page which is basically I'll get somebody to give me the email address first or whatever. Next page I'll be like hey this is this I'll tell them the story explain whatever it is I'm going to take you to the next page on that page the site's confusing it's going to be ugly you're going to be looking for this product you can click on this and tell exactly what to do and I have a bridge page where I explain exactly what they need to do and then this page I send them over to where they need to be. Or if it's a bridge page it can be like hey you just opted in for my coaching program now you got to go schedule a call. Like it's bridging them to where they're at and where you need them to go to. So we're just good at network marketing we're just good at coaching we're just good at connecting two different funnels like these bridge pages I use them all over the place. The goal is basically like here's where you're at this is where you need to go and it's bridging the gap. One way that I use these a lot as well is when I'm selling to like really really cold traffic. For example if I go to really cold traffic I have no idea who I am a lot of times one of the offers I do is I give away a free website like hey come get a free website. So somebody will come in they'll get a free website they opt in for free website because they know that word website and they'll come here and this page is like cool you're here to get your free website. This website is not a normal website this website is actually a thing that we call a funnel and it's so cool I'm going to give it to you for free and I explain what that is and I'm bridging this gap between what they understand which is a website and a funnel they don't understand I bridge that gap and they're like oh cool a funnel is just a better type of website cool I want that and I take them here to go and actually get the thing. So sometimes it's bridging concepts between people as well. So there's a lot of ways you use bridge pages I said they're really really powerful and there's so many different ways you can use bridge pages. Next here is a share page basically share pages after someone has gone through and they've opted in the next page just saying you should share this right. So example this is a couple ones so thank you so much for buying it's like oh share it like a groupie shout out like a anyway I thought these were kind of funny so share like a groupie you're getting people who just opt in or just bought something to share this with their friends. So then you're like hey you bought this guitar fingers don't keep it yourself share it with other people. So that's what the share page is thanking them and asking them to go and share it with their friends their followers and people that they know and that is what a share page is. Alright now we've gone through this someone's opted in they've gone through a bridge page they've given you an email address you've thanked them you may bridge them over to the next thing and now the next thing here is the sales page is how we're going to actually sell people stuff. Now sometimes again sometimes we may skip steps I might go from a squeeze page to a video sales and I might not have a thank you page so just remember as we'll see in here like that's why they're laggled you can move things around and figure out what's going to work the best for you. Okay so sales pages so the first type of sales page is a video sales letter that's this block right here in the video sales letter a couple things they're basically really simple again if you come back to like the recipe analogy there's like there's an ingredient here is one there one there and one there and that's it so it's really fast and easy okay but this is what a video sales letter someone comes in they watch a video of you selling your product your service and they click on a button and then they're taken over to a shopping cart very simple saying they watch a video here's the things they can buy at the end of it you click on it and they go over video sales letters for probably the last man eight or nine years has been the biggest thing ever so I'm sure you guys have seen a lot of them but that's basically what a video sales letter is and they work awesome okay alright the next one here is an actual sales letter okay. Now sales letters these come initially from kind of offline when I first got in this business when I was a kid I used to get direct mail in the mail all the time with long form sales letters I get a thing in the mail it opened they'd be like 40 or 50 pages selling me on a product or service I remember reading this little kid like being fascinated that that concept still works and it transitioned online and before video sales letters this was kind of what everyone did with actual actual sales letters so this is a this is a really cool sales that was basically long form telling a story about whatever it is and then sales letter trying to sell the product or service okay. Sales letters they still work a lot of times people think oh well this is old only video sales letters work but I found I think text sales letters are working better and better because people are more likely nowadays to scroll on their phone and read sometimes they're in the airport or the bathroom or whatever as opposed to watching a video so it's a really good way to get your information and do it through a through sales letter that way so that's kind of what a long form sales letter looks like and then the last one we're talking about here is is a product launch page so product launch page these are made famous by Jeff Walker basically it's he calls them a sideways sales letter so they're only long form sales letter you tip it on side and then he breaks it up over multiple days so somebody comes in and they they watch the first video and they watch the second video the third video and the fourth video he sells on the thing right he's made this process pretty famous but it's called a product launch and the product launch page is usually pretty simple there's a video there's place people that comments and there's usually like here's the videos that are coming up in the in the near future but again guys this page is simple it's logo four images there's a row there with an headline element there's a video with the video like they're super easy to start building when you realize the the page structure like we talked about earlier and those are sales pages alright the next one we'll talk about now are the actual order forms okay so first type of order form first one is like a traditional order form this is what most people outside of ClickFunnels world see all the time every single day it's a traditional one right so these are examples of traditionally I like shipping address the billing the credit card the product options are guarantees all sorts of stuff like that this is dollar shave clubs there's really simple I love it as well there's more of a traditional order form right and again these are simple to make inside of ClickFunnels but there's all these things like around the order form that mean they have the mean a big deal like having these kind of things increases conversions I wish we had another four hours I could talk about conversion I don't have time but this is kind of a traditional order form can look like okay alright the next one here is a two step order form this is one that we kind of popularized here inside of ClickFunnels and it works amazingly well basically the concept of it somebody comes here and on the first page of the order form basically asked for the shipping information like hey where do you want to ship this product and service to so this is the one we did for FiberFix they came here and said hey I want to I want to buy this this is offers really good I want that I'm shipping here so they put in their shipping excuse me they put in their shipping address you click on the button and then it takes them to the next section of pages okay like they can pick their product option like what do you want one two three six like people could pick that and they put their credit card and they have a little thing called an order form bump here they can put in there which increases your average car value I can't go into all those things tonight but this is basically a two step order form page looks like I don't typically do so much for like high ticket products but for low ticket products it looks really really good especially free plus shipping thing physical products because it takes out sometimes there's a lot of intimidation when someone sees like a big credit card order form like oh like the more traditional website there's so many fields it can be really really intimidating a lot of times the conversion will drop because of that where this is like simple it's like hey where do you want to ship it to you they're like oh they fill it in and they go to the next step and then they they've already made the commitment at this point and then here that's easier for them to put in their credit card the other cool thing about two step order forms if someone fills in step one but not step two you can target them and have whole email sequences going out to get them to fill in step number two so it's really really powerful tool the next one here this comes back to just a lot of the testing stuff we do this is a video sales letter page like we talked about right here but has the order form on the page most of the time for me I like having the order form especially video sales that are actually on the page so some of the video they click on the button that was there and then the order form appears down below on the page okay so it's just like a video sales letter it's like an order form just put on the same page most of the time in my for my experience having that will be higher conversion than having a video sales that are pushing them to an order form so that's why I have that as an order form next one is very similar it is the sales letter plus the order form same concept as well as that basically you've got a sales letter instead of clicking and going to different order form page you actually have the order form on the sales letter to scroll down okay so it's very similar next one here is the product launch plus order form this one is basically what happens is in a typical product launch there's three or four different videos right video number one is going to be a traditional where we go product launch page video two will be a product launch page three or three video four will be the last video in the series and the order form will be down below it so if you do a traditional product launch it's basically it's three of these pages than one of these pages okay so last page in the sequence is the is the product launch page the next step now we're shift to are the upsell pages okay so the first one is we call it OTO or one time offer very simple right so the first OTO page is basically we're taking somebody and said hey wait you just bought this first thing I got a really special offer for you what we do is we're gonna sell you six more bottles at a discount or whatever it is right this is one of our upsells for old supplement we had okay so there's an example of an OTO page wait before you go on I got a really special offer for you this is for fiberfix one we did where they just bought one kit and then basically we said hey you got a kit for yourself you need to go in the garage for the office for your friends they make great Christmas gifts we've got another thing for especially discount you get two more kids or you get one more kit and more offers here it's upsell converts so good now one thing I want to talk about is a little bit of upsell psychology for you with physical products almost always the upsell will be more of the same thing they just bought okay they bought something they're gonna sell more of that something they bought they bought fiberfix we're gonna sell more fiberfix like physical products almost 100% of the time my upsell will be more of the same thing they just bought okay information products that is the exact opposite okay and information product upsell I always want to sell them the next thing because they if they bought a book on how to lose weight even though they don't have the book yet in their mind like they solved that problem they figured out how to lose weight so the next thing I have to sell them has to be whatever strength camp it's got to be the next thing right so physical products I'm selling more of the same information products I'm selling the next thing so I just solved this problem for them by then buying this product what's the next thing that they need to accomplish and that's what the next upsell is going to be okay it's all about the strategy of what order those things are going in okay and again basically it's usually a video some copy and a button saying yes and then usually there's a no thanks button or he's saying no I don't actually want this okay a couple of things on this the no thanks links we always try to put like a negative qualifier so I don't say no thanks go log me in the members area now that's like a positive thing like oh cool I do want to go in the members area I say no thanks you hate no thanks I hate huge discounts on awesome stuff click here and they're like ah and it dramatically increases people saying yes because they don't want to say like I hate awesome stuff okay sounds funny but it really works alright next is a down so page so a down so page is basically if someone says no to this I can either take them to another upsell or I can end the funnel or I can down sell them and what we do down so page is typically like they don't buy the first thing then I'll say hey how about this we give you a payment plan and we'll down sell them a payment plan or down set the say they didn't say yes to the physical product I might down sell them a digital product or they didn't want six bottles of supplement I down to them one bottle okay so down so page is you take in that whatever you have and this changing the offer so it becomes cheaper form usually my video my page is like hey the only logical reason you possibly because a no to the sauce offer because it was so good you can't afford I totally understand that so I'm gonna do something give you a payment plan or I'm gonna give you a way to get the digital version or whatever that is that's kind of how a down sell page works alright so we're getting closer on all these pages you're gonna be quizzed at the end of these make sure you know all of them maybe alright the next ones here are webinar pages so these ones right now these are kind of been in order right like some go through pre-sell page then they give you the email address when you thank them then we're taking the video sells that I may have an order for them in an upsell these ones are different this this one right here like webinar pages could be at the front of a funnel it could go pre-sell page directly webinar right or someone ops or you know whatever that might be so these ones kind of skip they might be different order and I'll show you that here as we actually break down the funnels our first one is webinar registration page this is where somebody comes and actually register for the webinar right pretty simple example of one is this what I found webinar registration pages the more curiosity the better this headline my weird niche funnel is currently making me seventeen thousand nine hundred forty seven dollars per day and how to ethically knock it off in less than ten minutes what in the world am I talking about there like who knows like it's it's curiosity the best way to get people to show up to an event is giving so much curiosity they can't figure out the answer on their own webinars die when someone reads the headline they're like oh he's probably talking about this they may register they're not going to show up like curiosity is the key that's why we saw we put videos on our webinar registration pages then we start testing them I've never yet had a video on a webinar registration page beat out like a weird image that's just like what is Russell doing hang loose whoo like it's weird right it causes raw curiosity videos people typically give too much and they try to try to pitch a wise to me good and like it just it kills it so for me read webinar registration pages the more curiosity the better all right next page after they have registered for the webinar and they take new confirmation page where you're like hey thanks so much for registering for the webinar this was happening and so on the webinar registration page a couple things you can do you can come in here you can give them a you can sell them a product you can remind the webinar a bunch of different things you can do you can push them into another funnel so for me like someone will come here I thank them and then if the webinar is happening in the future I'll say hey click over here to go to this other funnel to get a free trial of click funnels or whatever that thing might be so you can push them to another funnel here on the registration page or you can just it can just end right there as well that's confirmation page next one is the broadcast room so after they've registered after they actually come in the webinar if this is an automated webinar you'll have a broadcast room if you're doing a live webinar you're probably using some third-party service like go to webinar webinar jam webinar zoom or uh what's zoom anyway some third party service if you're using an automated webinar then click phones actually has broadcast rooms where we'll stream these so they seem like they're live they're not live they'll seem like they're live you can come in they can watch it and it works really good so this is an example of a broadcast room and then a replay room is the last one so after they watched the webinar and didn't watch it in the follow-up sequence we'll send them a replay room they can go watch the webinar if they missed it okay in this example of what a replay room might look like they watch like hey you missed the webinar the replay is going to be live for the next 48 hours come watch it here and then come watch the actual replay okay those are all the webinar pages so you guys master those now next up are the membership pages so um first one is membership access page this is um where you actually someone would create their account if they're creating a membership site pretty simple the next one is the actual members area so someone logs in the membership site and then this is the members area so again these are all just click-front elements there's an element for side navigation there's an element for the content block they use plug-in three or four elements you got membership site and then you can go in there you can plug in all the training for each of the different tabs and there you got your members area which is all built together next one here are affiliate pages so if you're running an affiliate program in any of your funnels and you should all you do is you add in these two pages to every single funnel now you instantly got an affiliate program added that fast so first affiliate access page which basically is where the affiliate signs up so they can create join your affiliate program and then the next page is the affiliate area which is where you give them access to like their link so here's their link to promote your product in the funnel here's how much money they're going to make and you can go through you can give them sample sales tools you can give them things like that you can show them their stats all stuff are just elements that work inside of inside the affiliate area now both the membership pages and the affiliate pages all the elements for these pages only work on these pages so they only appear if you're on a membership page then those things pop up and then you can go and do them then the last thing we're going to do are some of the other pages these are pages we're going to use in some of the funnels so just here's a whole bunch of them we'll kind of go into them first one here is an application page if you're selling really expensive high ticket stuff I recommend having people apply so go through them they have an application page right the next one here is if you are in e-commerce people always want a storefront page because we're used to seeing that kind of style and so what a storefront page is basically it's a page so when you come they can see all the products you have but if someone clicks on the link it takes them into an actual funnel so it's kind of like a page that all of our e-commerce people like because it makes them feel like they're in e-commerce or they're selling that way but it pushes them into different funnels so that's kind of what a storefront page looks like next one is the home page again this comes back from all of our desire to have a web page that looks like the traditional web builders from from the 1980s and so that's what a home page is basically it's a page where you can be like hey here's who I am click here to go into one of my funnels here and here's something else I sell and click here to go into that funnel here's something else click here to go that it's just a page where you can feel good to brag about yourself and push people in the actual funnels will actually make you money a lot of times these are important because if you got like if you got a tv or something and your home page is a squeeze page the news won't take you serious we for years and click phones our home page was just a web and registration page made us way more money but we couldn't get on a legitimate publicity so we had to open a home page so that the pr people would think we're real business so there's reasons for it they're not good reasons but there are a couple reasons why you might want to use home page next is a live demo page all right live demo page probably the best example is every single week we do a thing called funnel Fridays we're basically we do a live demo of our software and then on there we got links so they can buy other products and services we have all the back archives of all the shows on it and that's kind of live demo page I'll show you some really cool ways you guys can use this here in the future the next one is an ask page I'd be careful how I pronounce that this is an ask page we're basically I'm going to ask people something this is how we get all of our market research data I want to have a potato gun product this is my idea to launch my pornography addiction product is what we did we launched two comic book coaching it's what we did like over and over and over again we don't just assume we know what people want we ask them so hey I make a potato gun product what's your number one biggest question you have about how to make potato guns and people told us and I found out what they wanted and then we created a product based on that so these are really good for figuring out what people want and so you create the right things there's an ask page next one here is a hero page we call it hero page because we have really cool badge and the new click book funnels onboarding it has batman on it looks awesome so the hero page is basically a page where you can brag about yourself because everyone likes to brag about themselves so if you're Dan Henry looks like this I build companies in batman underwear yeah so if you go to Russell Brunson dot com I have a hero page a lot of people have a hero page just like here's who I am here's my bio here's my social media here you can follow me that's what hero pages mostly vanity for all of us there's no real legitimate reason other than that so there's your hero page next one is an indoctrination page this is actually the last one the last one is an indoctrination page indoctrination pages are pages I send people someone will opt in to a squeeze page or register for webinar or whatever and I'll email them links to pages where I'm indoctrinating them on things okay so summer register for webinar I recorded a bunch of these really cool videos with Mandy that indoctrinate people in what we're doing between when they register for the webinar and when they show up for the webinar okay we're about to launch a new campaign a new version of ignite your funnel with somebody will opt in to squeeze page and then basically they get an indoctrination page every single day for the foreseeable future with me teaching them cool stuff and then pushing them somewhere teaching cool stuff pushing them somewhere that's what indoctrination pages is you'll see we use those in a lot of different places as well and there you guys are all of the page types inside of the funnel hacker cookbook now before I move on the one thing I want to mention is that every single just like every every one of these pages is consisting of four or five elements every single funnel is consisting of four or five of these so now you kind of know what they all are you understand the strategy behind each of them like when we start building funnels it's literally like okay I need one of these here I need one of this here I'm gonna have one of these and then one of here and there's my funnel like that's literally how this how this can become now that you understand the elements so on the pages and then the pages and then we're gonna talk about the pages that go into the funnels no matter where you are today in your business and in your life you're just one funnel away
